constraints: Each symbol of S is a digit from 0 to 9
content.inputFormat: Input contains a single line consisting of a string S of length N - the sequence of digits.
content.outputFormat: In a single line print a single integer - the minimal number of jumps Chef needs to reach digit SN from digit S1.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Chef can directly jump from the first digit (it is 0) to the last (as it is also 0).
Chef should follow the following path: 1-2-4-5-15.
Chef is already at the last digit.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use a Breadth-First Search (BFS) algorithm. We start from the first digit and try to reach the last digit in the minimum number of jumps. We can jump to the previous or next digit, or to any digit with the same value. We use a queue to keep track of the digits to visit and a set to keep track of the visited digits.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) - where N is the length of the string S. This is because in the worst case, we might need to visit all digits in the string.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) - where N is the length of the string S. This is because we need to store all digits in the queue and the set.

code.Python3:
from collections import deque

s = input()
n = len(s)
jumps = 0
visited = [False] * n
q = deque([0])
visited[0] = True
while q:
    size = len(q)
    for _ in range(size):
        u = q.popleft()
        if u == n - 1: break
        if u > 0 and not visited[u - 1]:
            q.append(u - 1)
            visited[u - 1] = True
        if u < n - 1 and not visited[u + 1]:
            q.append(u + 1)
            visited[u + 1] = True
        for j in range(n):
            if s[j] == s[u] and not visited[j]:
                q.append(j)
                visited[j] = True
    jumps += 1
print(jumps)
